Năm lớp 7, tôi từng bị một nhóm bạn lợi dụng. Ngoài ra còn bị họ lừa nữa. Bị mất tranh, tranh tôi từng vẽ 6 năm trước bị người khác thief rồi up lên Facebook 😢 và họ nói tranh tôi từng vẽ là tranh họ vẽ😡😢tức huhu

Tôi từng bị một người bạn cùng lớp lấy tranh của tôi đem bán trong khoá cấp 2 năm 2014-2018, họ còn cố tình nói thiếu thông tin về vụ đó nữa. Tới năm nay 2021 tôi mới biết tranh của tôi bị trace và thief luôn 😢

You'll most likely be forming an emotional connection with your NFT and this will help you to take some profits without exiting the art.

11/ You are not as smart as you think

You overestimate your own abilities. You get lucky a few times, and think you're smarter than you really are.  
Avoid overconfidence bias with:
Think of the consequences. Reflect on your mistakes. Pay attention to feedback. Learn 6/ https://t.co/diAHEiM0DQ

10/ Floor

Start to buy at the floor. Market price is known, most liquid to exit if needed.
When you want to buy Mid/Upper, be careful. There are many ways to calculate rarity and pure statistical rarity is often wrong. There are less buyers and market price is more subjective.

Look for projects that have a strong distribution. It's a sign of a sustainable community that's behind your JPEG.

For example, @BoredApeYC has 10K Items and 6.3K Owners, which is considered an extremely strong distribution. https://t.co/lkryRxWnUr
Just like with non-JPEG bets, look for innovation and strong builders/artists who are here for the long term.

9/ Project

The first job is to just browse, browse, browse to learn what you like and to understand the project. Check last sales, learn the traits, study the floor.
The number of JPGs you can buy will exceed your budget. So there is no reason to ever buy JPGs you don't like.

8/ Just tell me what to do

This is your natural tendency to follow the leader. Once you believe someone is an expert, you trust them. "They're the expert, they must be right!"  
Everybody can be wrong. Everybody can have own motives. 
Do not trust anybody, except ₿onzo.🙃

7/ Herd Mentality 

You have the tendency to follow and copy what others are doing. You are largely influenced by emotion and instinct, rather than by your own independent analysis.  
If you've ever felt FOMO, it could be because of herd mentality. Go back to 4/ https://t.co/wL5xymrUcM

6/ Confirmation

You only seek out information that tells you what you want to hear. You only follow people that say good things about project X. You unfollow and block anyone that spreads “FUD.”  
When you invest, seek and research the FUD to see if it's valid. https://t.co/njUOmXtHNA
Join Discord and ask questions, get active in the community.

5/ Connect

Follow the right people on Twitter.
By simply using the #NFT, #nftart, or #NFTCommunity hashtags, you’ll be finding yourself down the NFT Twitter rabbit whole where the NFT community is extremely active.

A good approach is one that investors would typically use to measure success for startups. 

Search for:
➖ Traction (social, discord, influencers, etc.)
➖ Team (people invested in the success of the project)
➖ Sales (# of sales and price movement in recent days/weeks)

4/ Learn

If you are going to invest money into something, it only make sense that you spend time learning. The NFT space takes a bit of time but if you are willing to put in some extra efforts your chances of finding good projects increase dramatically:

3/ First purchase

Learn how to use your @MetaMask  wallet, how to use @opensea / @LooksRareNFT .
The first NFT you should buy is a very cheap one.
Find one that you like and buy it when GAS is low.
If you like the experience - go on.

2/ Isolate 

Spend only money which is ok for you to go to zero.
Isolate your NFT investments from IRL, when all NFT go to $ 0, you need to have the exact same life like now.

1/ Disclaimer

₿onzo will not tell you which NFT to buy and doesn't really care which NFT you buy. 

DYOR

Now it is a good time to jump on some #nft blue chip projects with a a lot of research and without FOMO. 

₿onzo doesn't want you to FOMO beyond your financial capacity. Spend time learning, put in the extra effort.

6/

RIGHT CLICK SAVE - Non-NFT person thinking. Do not understand the value of NFTs
RUG - Team behind a project abandon the project and run away with money
SECONDARY - After mint, NFT can be sold on the secondary market
SER - means „sir“
WAGMI – We All Gonna Make It
WEN - When

5/

NFA - Not Financial Advice
NOOB - Newcomer, be fine to them!
OG - Original Gangster. Early in a project
OS - Opensea, most important platform
PAPER HANDS - Opposite of diamond hands. Panic seller
PFP - Profile picture
PROBABLY NOTHING - means probably something

4/

IRL - In Real Life
JPGs - We love JPGs
LFG - Let’s Fucking Go
LL - Larvalabs are the creators of the punks
LOOKS RARE - Rarity is a driver of value. Often used ironically.
McDonalds - Our backup career plan
MINT - Creation of NFT
TO THE MOON - Price goes up

3/

FLOOR SWEEPING - People keep buying NFTs which are listed at floor
FOMO Fear Of Missing Out
FREN - means „friend“
FUD - Fear, Uncertainty and Doubt. People tend to panic sell.
GM - Good Morning
GMI - Gonna Make It
GN - Good night
HODL - Hold On for Dear Life
a new NFT

2/

💎🙌🏻 - Diamond hands refers to someone who don‘t panic sell
DYOR - Do Your Own Research
FLIP - Buy an asset cheap and sell it fast with profit
FLOOR PRICE - lowest price for specific collection / item
FLOOR IS LAVA - Floor price is raising fast

1/

AIRDROP - free distribution for project holders
APE IN - Buy into a NFT project
ATH - All Time High
BAYC - Bored Ape Yacht Club
COPE - Opposite of FOMO
DEGEN - People who invest in NFT without research
DELIST - Cancel the listing of an NFT

This is a 🧵 for all NFT twitter noobs, explaining our language/wordings 📖.

We have a great culture. Positive, friendly and helpful. Let's keep it this way!
